target.name: Arbe Robotics
target.founder: Kobi Marenko, Noam Arkind, Oz Fixman

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Arbe Robotics is a public-stage drone and autonomous systems company that has raised $118M to date. The valuation universe for this sector comprises six private peers—including Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Elroy Air—with no direct public comparables available. The narrow peer set and heterogeneity of business models (air taxi, autonomous delivery, logistics) within the Drones & Autonomous sector create meaningful constraints on traditional multiples-based valuation.

The peer-median method is not applicable to Arbe given the sparse comparable population. The sector contains only five firms with sufficient data visibility, but filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yields fewer than the three datapoints required for robust median analysis. An all-sector median would obscure material differences in unit economics, regulatory pathways, and commercialization timelines across these sub-segments.

Without quantitative triangulation methods producing defensible results from the available data, a valuation conclusion cannot be responsibly generated from this dataset. The analyst would require either: (1) additional comparable company financials (revenue, profitability, capital efficiency) to weight within the existing peer set; (2) transaction comps or secondary market pricing on Arbe itself; or (3) explicit management guidance on near-term revenue and capital requirements. The primary uncertainty remains the absence of sufficient granular peer metrics to anchor a range.
